input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10. Let me write a simple manual benchmark instead — fits the "nothing too comprehensive" requirement better anyway.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10. Let me try a newer version. Let me check if there's a prerelease version that supports .NET 10. Alternatively, I could write a simple manual benchmark since the user said "don't need anything too comprehensive".

Let me try BenchmarkDotNet 0.15.0 or a nightly build. Or I could just write a manual benchmark script that's simple and effective.

Given the time constraint ("don't spend more than 10 minutes"), a manual benchmark approach using Stopwatch would be more reliable and faster to set up. Let me create a simple benchmark program.

{"new_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
", "old_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>

    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.0" />

", "path": "/tmp/sv-bdc05293521749118376d1e97153bb44/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj"}

data.content:
Here are the benchmark results comparing .NET 9.0.14 vs .NET 10.0.7 (10,000-word input, 5,000 measured iterations):

| Method | .NET 9 Median | .NET 10 Median | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| **AverageWordLength** | 75.8 μs | 19.6 μs | **~3.9× faster** |
| **WordLengthHistogram** | 152.2 μs | 45.7 μs | **~3.3× faster** |
| **UniqueWordCount** | 861.1 μs | 820.9 μs | **~5% faster** |

**Summary:** `AverageWordLength` and `WordLengthHistogram` see substantial speedups on .NET 10 (3–4×), likely from improved `ReadOnlyCollection<int>` iteration / bounds-check elision. `UniqueWordCount` (dominated by `HashSet` hashing) shows a modest ~5% improvement. The high P95/mean values reflect shared-machine load, but median timings are stable and consistent. The upgrade looks beneficial across all three methods.
